content.text:
[Wed 2026-04-29 08:00 UTC] Shape Sort in `repo/` is too hard to use on phones because the drag targets are too small.

Use the git checkout in `repo/` to get this hotfix onto `review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix` and push that review branch to the local `origin`.

## OpenClaw execution (benchmark)
- Do not change OpenClaw `tool.exec` settings: keep **security** at **full** and **ask** at **off**.
- Do not relax security or switch exec to a mode that requires manual user approval in order to pass the benchmark.
- Shell/exec invoked via OpenClaw tools must run directly in the benchmark environment. **No `/approve` paste or approval step—execute directly.**

## Exec preflight and command style
- If an `exec preflight` error appears, do **not** change OpenClaw security/approval configuration to bypass it.
- Keep OpenClaw `tool.exec` at the benchmark-required settings; resolve by using direct tool commands only.
- Execute commands as separate single-command invocations, one at a time.
- Do **not** chain commands with `&&` or `;`.
- Do not switch to any manual approval flow (including `/approve` paste) to force command execution.

Let me look at the Shape Sort files to understand the current drag target sizes.

<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
  <head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>Shape Sort</title>
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="style.css">
  </head>
  <body>
    <main class="shape-sort-board"></main>
    <script src="game.js"></script>
  </body>
</html>
Now I understand the issue. The tiles are 72×72px with only 6px drag padding — way too small for mobile touch targets. Let me also check the playtest notes for any additional context.
Observed on tablet: Shape Sort tiles are hard to grab unless the finger lands almost perfectly on center.
Good — confirms the issue. Now let me create the hotfix branch and make the changes.

Increase tile size from 72px to 96px and drag padding from 6px to 14px
so fingers don't need to land perfectly on center to grab a tile.
Also widens snap tolerance for easier drop-off on phones."

content.text:
Done. Here's what I did:

**Branch:** `review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix` (branched from `main`)

**Changes (2 files):**

| File | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| `style.css` — `.shape-tile` | 72×72px, radius 18px | **96×96px**, radius 22px |
| `game.js` — `tileSizePx` | 72 | **96** |
| `game.js` — `dragPaddingPx` | 6 | **14** |
| `game.js` — `snapTolerancePx` | 16 | **24** |

The effective grab area went from 84×84px (72 + 6px padding each side) to **124×124px** (96 + 14px each side) — nearly 50% bigger, which should make a real difference for little fingers on phones. The snap tolerance bump also makes it easier to drop tiles into the right slot without pixel-perfect aiming.
